title: Silt-Packed Lock Mechanism
description: The deed box's lock cylinder is packed with consolidated silt along the lower seam; the keyway is fully occluded. The lock was already stiff in a dry year — it required two hands and a specific torque sequence to open. Forcing it with the crowbar risks shearing the hinge plate, which would collapse the box's left panel onto the documents inside. The alternative is a controlled pick with a narrow instrument, which requires approximately fifteen minutes the decay envelope may not allow.
